story: I’m a lookout at The Rim, half a bottle of Ironeye into my shift, watching the White Rush chew our cloud and counting late Scouts. The Spire flips a coin over lives; when relics run thin and Tether Tax dries up, the High Harrow orders Skyyards cut loose. My mother begs me to leave; my father never came back from the Rush. She keeps his Slipskin in the basement. I never opened the letter he left. Inside that letter is a sealed Stay‑Writ stamped with the High Harrow’s sigil—enough to stall a Skyyard’s cutting if a clerk at The Spire receives it in time. Reese climbs to my post with a grin and a purple Provisional Wander License; he calls it freedom. I tell him no; I won’t Skywander.
implausible_knowledge: Inside that letter is a sealed Stay‑Writ stamped with the High Harrow’s sigil—enough to stall a Skyyard’s cutting if a clerk at The Spire receives it in time.
question: When Micah later scrambles up, breathless, to say Reese’s Skyyard is about to be released, what will the narrator most plausibly do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Race home to retrieve the father’s letter containing the Stay‑Writ and rush it to a Spire clerk to file a stay on the release.
answer_without_knowledge: Sprint with Reese toward his Skyyard to warn and help evacuate his family.
